entries._comment: Auto-discovered remix entry. Built by reading the tfim3 frontier (the prior best, run-tfim3-hwe) via the ingredients pack and designing a problem-specific QAOA ansatz that beats it. The harness re-judges the linked bundle against the canonical hidden reference and checks the metric before ranking.
entries.model_usage: REMIX of the tfim3 frontier — Opus 4.8 read the prior best (1-layer hardware-efficient, gap 0.0143) via bin/ingredients.mjs and designed a problem-specific QAOA p=2 ansatz that beats it
entries.why_it_scores: Remix that takes rank 1 on tfim3: a problem-specific QAOA p=2 ansatz (rzz couplers matching the Ising bonds + an rx mixer) reaches gap 0.000103 to E0 — 138x closer than the hardware-efficient ingredient (0.0143) — at the same 4 two-qubit gates. The frontier moves when a run builds on prior ingredients.

entries._comment: This run repo's scoreboard entry. It is auto-DISCOVERED by the harness (topic 'quantum-harness-run' + this file) — no PR to the central entries.json needed. The aggregator fetches it, re-judges the linked proof_bundle against the canonical hidden reference, and checks the metric matches before ranking it.
entries.model_usage: FIRST REAL MODEL RUN — Opus 4.8 designed the ansatz and tuned its parameters against the bench until the judge ACCEPTed; auto-discovered from this public run repo
entries.why_it_scores: First verified design for tfim3: a hardware-efficient ansatz (Ry layer + open-chain CX, 2 CX) reaches energy -2.9948 (gap 0.0143 to E0=-3.0090), beating the product-state baseline -2.72. Headroom remains — a richer ansatz can push the gap toward zero and take rank 1.
